title: Podman auto-update behavior for image-based volumes?
body:
### Feature request description

I'm referring to the feature discussed in #23775.

Is there a possibility to have `podman auto-update` *also* update (and restart the kube) when the image of an image-based volume is changed?

As a workaround, how would I go about it using `.image` quadlet files?

My use case is starting a service, as a container through Podman, by Systemd. The service requires additional configuration data which I'm managing via git, with automatic CI/CD creation of a data-only configuration data image. I'm using `podman auto-update` periodically and I'd want changes to the configuration data image to eventually propagate to the point where the service is restarted with new configuration.

So far, the workflow seems to be:
  - create a `config-data.image` file for the configuration data image
  - set up a `config-data-image.timer` for the image to be updated periodically
  - set up a `service.yml` k8s YAML file, make sure `--force` is used on service shutdown (so the volume doesn't persist)
  - set up a `service.kube` file which depends on `config-data-image.service` for its first run (so that the image gets automatically pulled if not present locally)
  - set up `podman-auto-update.timer` to a sensible value; this will take care of the service being restarted when the service execution image changes, but not when the config image changes (right?)

The problem with this is that I actually have no way of restarting a service *exactly when* the config image changes. I can re-download the image periodically if a newer version exists, but there's no way that I know of of conditionally triggering a restart of the application *if and only if a new version of configuration data is available.*

As to how the feature could look like: I'm not exactly sure (too inexperienced with Podman internals).

From a user's point of view, making an `ExtendendPullPolicy` or `ExendedAutoUpdate` key for the `.kube` file, which should apply not only to execution images, but to other resource images as well (specifically volume images) would fit the bill. But it somehow feels... "oddly specific" and migh not be good API design (?)

A very crude workaround for now would be to include the service config image as a container in its own right -- doing nothing besides a "sleep infiniy", but co-existing in the same Pod as the actual service. This would at least trigger a Pod restart on "podman auto-update" every time the config image is updated at the source. But this still doesn't guarantee that the new version of the image is actually *downloaded* before use.

Any ideas?

(Background: the larger context of this request is me trying to build structurally simple systems, with all the advantages of using pods, containers, "gitops" workflows, but without actually having to deploy full k8s setups. Using only a bare-metal CoreOS installation, and relying heavily on Podman, Systemd and Quadlets instead of Kubernetes, is a huge step in terms of complexity reduction without sacrificing modularity and separation of concerns.

### Suggest potential solution

_No response_

### Have you considered any alternatives?

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
